methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Joby Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $10B+

private.name: Archer Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $5B

data.narrative:
ideaForge is a public-stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector that has raised $50M+. The valuation universe for comparison is constrained: the Drones & Autonomous sector contains only four same-sector peers, all private, with no public comparables identified. The peer set includes Joby Aviation, Archer Aviation, Nuro, Elroy Air, and Aurora Innovation, though none satisfy the filtering criteria for reliable peer-median triangulation.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this analysis. A narrow peer set exists with only two same-stage companies and zero adjacent-stage or similar-tier comparables, falling short of the three-datapoint minimum required for statistical reliability. Expanding to an all-sector median would introduce significant methodology risk given the heterogeneity of business models and unit economics across the broader Drones & Autonomous landscape. No public trading comps exist to anchor valuation via EV/revenue or EV/EBITDA multiples.

Without sufficient comparable transactions or public market data, conventional triangulation methods cannot be reliably applied to ideaForge. The absence of public peers and the fragmented nature of the private market in this subsector limit the strength of any valuation conclusion. Additional analysis would require alternative methods such as DCF modeling with explicit assumptions regarding cash-flow timing and terminal growth, or reference to transaction multiples from the broader autonomous systems market. The current analytical framework does not support a quantified valuation range.
